title: Today's Tech Brief: Scaling creativity in the age of AI
summary: Exploring the intersection of technology and creativity in the age of AI
imageAlt: Image from source article for Scaling creativity in the age of AI
imageCaption: Scaling creativity in the age of AI
imageSourceTitle: Scaling creativity in the age of AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I'm looking at a fascinating piece from MIT Technology Review, which got me thinking about the role of AI in scaling creativity. The article, 'Scaling creativity in the age of AI,' explores how technology is changing the way we express ourselves and tell stories. It's a topic that's really close to my heart, and I'm excited to dive in.

The MIT Technology Review article highlights how storytelling is at the core of human DNA. It's how we share our ideals, warnings, hopes, and experiences. What's interesting is that technology has always played a role in medium and distribution. I am seeing a platform shift where AI is now a key enabler of creative expression. For instance, AI-powered tools can help with content generation, video editing, and even music composition.

Another news item that caught my attention is the rise of virtual influencers. These are AI-powered digital personas that can interact with humans in a lifelike way. They're being used in entertainment, marketing, and even education. It's amazing to think about the potential for virtual influencers to revolutionize the way we consume content and interact with each other online.

I'm also following the developments in AI-powered art. There are some incredible examples of AI-generated art that's almost indistinguishable from human-created art. It's raising interesting questions about authorship, creativity, and what it means to be an artist. As someone who's interested in technology and creativity, I find it fascinating to see where this intersection is headed.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
